cases.description: Basic Claude-compatible /v1/messages streaming request returns displayable text.

request.prompt: Reply with exactly this text: compatibility-basic-ok

cases.description: System prompt plus structured text content is accepted without gateway breakage.

request.system: You are a compatibility probe. Follow the user request exactly.

content.text: Reply with exactly this text: system-content-ok

cases.description: Usage fields exist and a longer prompt reports more input tokens than a shorter prompt.

request.prompt: Reply with exactly this text: usage-short-ok

secondary_request.prompt: Reply with exactly this text: usage-long-ok. Context: alpha beta gamma delta epsilon zeta eta theta iota kappa lambda mu nu xi omicron pi rho sigma tau upsilon phi chi psi omega. Repeat no context back.

cases.description: Streaming events expose start, text delta, usage delta, and stop events with latency evidence.

request.prompt: Write one concise sentence of at least twelve words about streaming compatibility. Do not use markdown.

cases.description: Strict JSON output is parseable without markdown wrapping and preserves field types.

request.prompt: Return exactly this JSON object and no other text: {"status":"ok","count":3,"items":["alpha","beta"]}

cases.description: Cache-control content blocks are accepted and cache usage fields are reported when supported.

content.text: Compatibility cache probe shared prefix. Reply with exactly this text: cache-probe-ok
Compatibility cache probe shared prefix. Reply with exactly this text: cache-probe-ok

cases.description: Tool schema is accepted and the stream exposes a tool_use trajectory.

request.prompt: Use the tool named report_compatibility_status with status set to ok and reason set to tool-call-probe.
